intro: Thrasya ansata, also known as thrasya or wild mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Thrasya ansata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Thrasya ansata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as to alleviate menstrual cramps and other female health issues.
